skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: The density of a certain type of cork is 224 kilograms per cubic meter. A cubical block of this cork has a mass of 215 kilograms. To the nearest hundredth of a meter, what is the length of one edge of this block?
rationale: Volume = mass / density = 215 / 224 ≈ 0.95982 cubic meters. For a cube V = s³, so s = ∛0.95982 ≈ 0.9865, which rounds to 0.99. Choice (c) is correct.
